geographic_scope: Jenin refugee camp and immediate environs
excerpt: HRW confirmed at least 52 Palestinian deaths and identified at least 22 civilians.
transform_note: Used as the Palestinian civilian-death numerator for the side-specific DR; treated as a minimum-confirmed count.
confidence_note: Strong field-investigation basis, but still a minimum because some status questions remained unresolved.

geographic_scope: Jenin refugee camp and immediate environs
excerpt: HRW identified at least 27 confirmed dead as suspected armed Palestinians; some were PA security personnel in armed roles.
transform_note: Mapped to the project's military/combatant umbrella, which includes organized armed personnel and PA security members in armed operational roles.
confidence_note: Strong for a minimum armed-personnel death count; some unresolved cases remain outside this total.

geographic_scope: Jenin refugee camp and immediate environs
excerpt: HRW retained three Palestinian deaths with unresolved civilian/combatant status.
transform_note: Kept separate from the base DR path under the prompt's strict unknown-status rule.
confidence_note: Explicit unresolved-status bucket; no silent allocation performed.

geographic_scope: Jenin refugee camp and immediate environs
excerpt: UN report states 13 IDF soldiers were killed in the 9 April ambush and a 14th elsewhere that day, bringing Jenin-operation deaths to 23.
transform_note: Recorded as a separate side-specific military death series; not merged into the Palestinian-side DR denominator path.
confidence_note: Strong convergence between UN summary and contemporary Israeli reporting on the 23-soldier death toll.

excerpt: Used as the pre-battle civilian ceiling for Jenin camp, not as the average exposed count throughout the battle.
transform_note: Serves as the high civilian-exposure scenario for the camp-internal DR path.
confidence_note: Good pre-battle population anchor, but not a direct measure of those remaining under fire throughout the battle.

excerpt: UN report records an Israeli-source account that about 11,000 inhabitants departed voluntarily.
transform_note: Used to justify a low-end remaining-civilian scenario near 4,000 and to reject a simple fixed 14,000 denominator.
confidence_note: Best treated as an upper-bound departure claim rather than a precise daily-exposure series.

excerpt: UN report says estimates vary but as many as 4,000 civilians may have remained in the camp throughout.
transform_note: Used as the low civilian-exposure scenario in the DR denominator range.
confidence_note: Useful as a floor/strict-exposure anchor, but still approximate.

excerpt: HRW relays media estimates that armed Palestinians in the camp numbered roughly 80-100.
transform_note: Used as the low and best militant-exposure anchor for the Palestinian-side DR path.
confidence_note: Weaker than the death-count evidence because it is a media-estimate context claim, not a roster.

excerpt: UN report preserves an IDF-spokesman portrayal that soldiers faced 'hundreds of gunmen.'
transform_note: Used only to justify a conservative high-end militant-exposure scenario of roughly 300; not treated as a point estimate.
confidence_note: Low-confidence upper-bound context because it reflects one side's characterization rather than neutral counting.

excerpt: Used to close the main battle window on 11 April for person-month calculations.
transform_note: Supports the 9-day battle window used in denominator calculations.
confidence_note: Adequate for episode timing even though some humanitarian consequences continued after 11 April.
